_meta.source_series: S2 — Building the Agentic Harness (core mechanics) + S1 B10-12 (chapter walkthroughs)
_meta.note: Mechanics RENDER cited S2 passages; walkthroughs are the /seeit/<book>-chN pages S1 B10-12 link to. Book↔code: sourced + hashed; re-run to auto-refresh.

topics.title: Receipts — the Verifiable Audit Chain
topics.operator_explanation: Every action the agents take produces a receipt — a real, verifiable audit-chain entry. Each receipt extends the chain, and every extension renews your presence in the loop: the system operates WITH your witness, not after a one-time approval. You can always prove what happened, and the book's own claims are checkable against the real receipts.

source.book: S2 Vol 3 — The Governed Dev Loop
source.section: Witness, Not Approval — What the Posture Means at the Config Layer
source.passage: This is structurally different from approval-only patterns. In approval-only systems, the operator's role disappears the moment approval is granted (the system runs on the artifact, not the operator's continuing presence). In witness-mode systems, the witness chain *is* the artifact — every act produces a receipt, every receipt extends the chain, every chain extension renews the operator's structural presence in the harness's operation. The harness operates *with* the operator's witness, not *after* the operator's approval.

topics.title: K1–K4 Constitutional Invariants
topics.operator_explanation: Four rules the system ALWAYS enforces, no exceptions. The first and most important — K1, human primacy — means you stay in command: agents propose, you dispose. Nothing material becomes action without your breath at the gate. The others guarantee receipts, fail-closed safety, and honest errors.

source.passage: The Authoritative Pattern Rule (documented in `breathline-federation/governance/decisions/2026-05-08_v0.6.0-horizon.md`) states: **no runtime ships ahead of the sealed companion book**. For the Atrium, this means the live cutover requires Series 1 Book 12 (*The Agentic Enterprise* — the constitutional invariants K1–K4) to be breath-sealed first. The mock can run anywhere; the live runtime can only ship behind the sealed book.

topics.title: Merkle Anchors (Receipted Memory)
topics.operator_explanation: Everything the system does is written to a tamper-evident chain — each entry locked to the one before it by a cryptographic hash (the Merkle layer). Like a sealed ledger: if anything were altered, the chain breaks visibly, verifiable against a public anchor. It's how you can PROVE what happened and when, years later.

source.section: P5 Merkle Integrity — B51 Entries Are Audit-Chainable
source.passage: Each B51 entry is a sealed cylinder with a P1 signature (P1 — `breathline-sealed/primitives/sealed/layer_1_root/sign.py`) and a Merkle anchor (P5 — `breathline-sealed/primitives/sealed/layer_5_shields/merkle_tree.py`). The P5 Merkle integrity is what makes the entry audit-chainable not just to the curator but to *every event the curator considered* — the Merkle tree's leaves are the considered-event hashes; the tree's root proves the curator's curation operated on the actual event set, not a tampered subset.

topics.title: The Governed Loop
topics.operator_explanation: How a change is governed: agents propose, and proposal mechanics gate every self-modification through a structural breath-gate review — with cross-role veto authority — before anything becomes action. The agents do the work in the background and bring you a single decision; nothing lands without your gate.

source.book: S2 Vol 3 — The Governed Dev Loop
source.passage: **The Capability.** The **governed dev loop** Vol 3 deep-dives flips this. **Operator-defined role configuration** (§1) ships blank with `role_spec.yaml` patterns the operator authors under their Charter; the platform prescribes nothing. **Proposal mechanics** (§2a) gate every agent self-modification through structural breath-gate review with cross-role veto authority. **The hard constitutional boundary** (§2b) is the structural commitment that GREEN auto-flow is *structurally barred* from touching the constitutional surface (K1-K4 invariants from Series 1 Book 12) — not by policy, by enforcement at the harness layer.

topics.title: Atrium Surfaces (The Cockpit)
topics.operator_explanation: The Atrium is your cockpit — one place to see everything: what the agents are doing, what the constitution is enforcing, what's waiting for your breath, and what's already sealed. You never leave it; every screen makes human primacy visible, so you stay sovereign without touching code.

source.section: Chapter 1: The Stillpoint — Sovereign Node Identity & Health
source.passage: **The Stillpoint is a lens, not a gate.** This distinction governs the entire cockpit and is structurally important to name early. Competing agentic-AI tools in 2026 ship interfaces that *look* like dashboards but *act* like gates — the operator clicks Approve in the dashboard, the dashboard enacts the approval. The Atrium inverts this. The constitution lives in the harness underneath; the cockpit renders the constitutional posture *visibly*. The Stillpoint shows you what the constitution is doing right now. The Breath-Gate (Chapter 2) is where the operator's approval *binds* — but the binding happens through a sealed receipt against the constitutional surface, not through the cockpit's button alone.

chapters.title: The cross-role veto that protects the deal
